rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form And Special Interrogatories, And To Request For Production Of Documents, That Facts Be Deemed Admitted, For Monetary Sanctions,
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 22, 2011, Line 3, DEFENDANT CHRIS PASTENA, JESSE BRANSTETTER, MICHAEL PASTENA, CULINARY ARTS STUDIOS LLC's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form And Special Interrogatories, And To Request For Production Of Documents, That Facts Be Deemed Admitted, For Monetary Sanctions. The requests for admissions are deemed admitted. rulings.Case Title: REBECCA BARGANS et al VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al

Granted in part. The deposition of UCSF¿s Custodian of Records already took place, Defendants shall provide further responses to requests for admissions 1 and by December 2, 2011. The objections to requests 1 and 2 lack merit. The response to request 2 is evasive. All requests for sanctions are denied. rulings.Case Title: CALVO & CLARK LLP, VS. DAVID J. LUJAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendant'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 22, 2011, Line 16, Cross-Defendant'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings IS Granted with leave to amend as to the first cause of action for legal negligence, the third cause of action for fraud in the inducement, the fourth cause of action for non-disclosure of material facts, the eighth cause of action for negligent misrepresentation and the tenth cause of action for accounting, sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for unjust enrichment and the ninth cause of action of abuse of process, and denied as to the second cause of action for breach of contract, the fifth cause of action for contractual bad faith, and the seventh cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty Mr. Lujan is given fifteen days leave to file an amended complaint to allege, if he can do so in good faith, that: 1) but for the alleged malpractice he would have obtained a more favorable result in any or all of the cases where he retained the Calvo firm to represent him (first cause of action), 2) the elements of his fraud claims with more particularity as what false representations were made to him, what facts were concealed from him and when and by whom the representations and concealments were made (third, fourth and eighth causes of action), and 3) an unknown balance due to him by the Calvo firm (tenth cause of action) Unjust enrichment and abuse of process are not cognizable claims. The second, fifth and seventh causes of action are adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS WALKER et al VS. PLATINUM CAPITAL GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 22, 2011, Line 3, DEFENDANT AURORA LOAN SERVICES' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the first, third, fourth, sixth and seventh causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the second and fifth causes of action. The first, third, fourth, sixth and seventh causes of action are properly pled. The second cause of action incorrectly applies the defense of impossibility. (Glendale Fed. Sav. & Loan Ass¿n v. Marin View Heights (1977) 66 Cal.App.3d 101, 154). Cancellation of a deed of trust is a remedy that is subsumed by the first and seventh causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS VS. ACE PROPERTY AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 22, 2011, Line 6, PLAINTIFF SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' Motion To Compel Further Production Of Documents. Granted in part. Even assuming that the work product privilege applies to communications with Genex about the value of services provided by plaintiff, it is a qualified privilege and there is compelling need by plaintiff to receive the documents reflecting those communications. However, defendant need not produce documents that do not relate to provision of services to the patient by providers other than plaintiff because those documents are not responsive to the requests at issue. The court did conduct an in camera review of the documents received from defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS VS. ACE PROPERTY AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Amended Mtn To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 22, 2011, Line 6, DEFENDANT ACE PROPERTY AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY's Second Amended Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents. Granted. The discovery sought meets the standard of discovery relevance and plaintiff has not satisfied its burden of showing that it should not be required to provide it on grounds of burden, confidentiality or any other reason. The prior order granting this motion which is reflected in the court¿s minutes of September 26, 2011 was entered in error and is vacated. rulings.Case Title: MELINDA B. CORTEZ et al VS. THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON FKA THE BANK OF NEW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 22, 2011, Line 6, DEFENDANTS THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON FKA THE BANK OF NEW AND RECONSTRUCT COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiffs are granted leave, if they can do so in good faith, to allege: (1) unconditional tender; (2) the terms of the contract or loan modification with more clarity; (3) that plaintiff is owed money and entitled to an accounting and restitution; and (4) a promissory estoppel claim. The wrongful foreclosure cause of action appears improper because no foreclosure actually occurred. A party can bring a UCL cause of action merely to recover restitution. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: EMIL P MILYAKOV et al VS. JP MORGAN CHASE, NA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary And Permanent Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 22, 2011, Line 9, PLAINTIFF EMIL MILYAKOV, MAGDALENA APOSTOLOVA's Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary And Permanent Injunction. This motion will be heard by Judge McBride on November 30, 2011 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 220 due to the CCP 170.6 challenge to Judge Kahn. It is the court¿s understanding that the foreclosure is set for December 7, 2011 so a temporary restraining order is unnecessary. If the sale is set on or before November 30, then, per Judge McBride, the sale is temporarily enjoined until the motion for preliminary injunction is heard. No court reporter will be provided for the hearing. If any party desires a court reporter, the parties should meet and confer to agree on a single reporter. = (302/HEK)
